Mila Resources Plc / Index: LSE / Epic: MILA / Sector: Natural Resources
8 June 2026
Mila Resources Plc
("Mila" or the "Company")
High Grade Intercepts Reported from Yarrol Gold Project:
Pre-Resource Evaluation and Preliminary Economic Study Phases to Commence
Mila Resources Plc (LSE: MILA), the resource development company focussed on gold and copper in Australia, is pleased to announce assay results from a further nine RC drill holes at its Yarrol Gold Project in Queensland, Australia, which enhance the scale and continuity of gold mineralisation. The results highlight the project's broader potential, with mineralisation remaining open in several directions and additional targets emerging across the project area.
The Company also provides an update on additional planned work programmes across its wider portfolio, including at the Monal Gold-Copper Project in Queensland, and the Kathleen Valley Gold Project in Western Australia.
Overview
Yarrol -
final assay results from holes MYARC0211-MYARC0214 and MYARC0217-MYARC0221 of the initial 12-hole RC drilling programme at Yarrol:
·
Continuity and extensions to depth confirmed, with several holes returning multiple gold intercepts that collectively define broad zones of mineralisation, including:

·
Continued identification of multiple high-grade gold shoots from shallow depth within a large mineralised system.
·
Broad lower grade halo shows potential to define significant tonnage in the system.
·
Continuity of mineralisation confirmed well beyond the historic resource footprint at Yarrol North and to greater depths.
·
The gold system remains open along strike and at depth, with additional high-grade targets and parallel mineralised structures yet to be tested, supporting potential for further growth.
·
Data integration and geological modelling work has commenced in preparation for resource estimation studies.
·
Data integration and geological modelling is expected to be completed by mid-July, informing the next stage of drilling scheduled to begin in August, with the aim of maximising the initial resource size.
Parallel work programmes to advance broader portfolio:
·
Induced Polarisation (IP) geophysical studies to commence across Monal to generate and better define drill gold-copper porphyry targets.
·
Strategic review of Kathleen Valley to commence with a low-cost, desktop-led Mineral Resource Estimate upgrade, to demonstrate the asset's underlying value and support potential value-realisation initiatives.
Mark Stephenson, Executive Chairman, commented:
"The completion of this RC drilling programme at Yarrol marks an important milestone in the project's progression towards an initial Mineral Resource Estimate. With our current bank of drill results showing excellent intercepts from shallow depth, we are now moving decisively into the resource definition phase with confidence. Geological modelling and data integration work is already underway and further updates expected throughout 2026.
"Our immediate focus is to establish a robust geological and structural framework that will support a future resource estimate and provide the foundation for the subsequent economic feasibility studies. The drilling completed to date has consistently demonstrated the scale and continuity of the Yarrol gold system, while also confirming that mineralisation remains open in multiple directions. These results reinforce our belief that Yarrol has the potential to develop into a significant and valuable gold asset, and we are excited to begin quantifying that scale of opportunity through the next stage of technical work.
"At the same time, we continue to advance the broader portfolio. At Monal, preparations are underway for an IP geophysics programme designed to refine and prioritise a pipeline of gold-copper porphyry targets ahead of drilling. At Kathleen Valley in Western Australia, we intend to commence a desktop review and Mineral Resource Estimate upgrade programme with external consultants, ensuring that we continue to unlock value across all three of our key projects."
Yarrol Gold Project - Transition to Resource Evaluation
Completion of Initial Drilling Programme
The Company's 12-hole RC drill programme was designed to test the continuity, grade distribution and geological controls of mineralisation across key target areas at Yarrol. The results have successfully demonstrated a coherent gold system characterised by high-grade cores within a broader envelope of moderate-grade mineralisation.
Figure 1 Cross section through MYARC0213, at the northern extent of the Yarrol North prospect
Geological Interpretation and Scale
The evolving geological model suggests that mineralisation is structurally controlled, with multiple channels of higher-grade mineralisation developing at repeating intersections of structures within a broader mineralised corridor.
This style of gold system is considered highly prospective for both tonnage and grade and supports the Company's interpretation that Yarrol represents a meaningful emerging gold system.
Importantly, mineralisation remains open in multiple directions at relatively shallow depth, indicating there is considerable scope to build the resource inventory by future drilling campaigns.
Pre-Resource Phase
With drilling now complete, Mila has commenced a pre-resource review phase.
During this stage, the Company's technical team will:
·
Integrate all drilling, geological and structural datasets
·
Refine the 3D geological model
·
Evaluate controls on grade distribution and shoot geometry
This phase represents a key transition point from exploration-led drilling towards resource definition and future economic assessment,
building on the 3 drilling programmes completed by Mila in the last 18 months.
​
​
Figure 2 Overview of drilling at Yarrol and significant high-grade and wide intercepts from Mila's drilling (yellow) and historic drilling (white)
Monal Gold-Copper Project - IP Programme Commencement
Alongside its pre-resource evaluation work at Yarrol, Mila is advancing its Monal Project, targeting gold-copper porphyry-style mineralisation.
Geological mapping and rock chip sampling is underway at key targets, with samples already submitted to the laboratory for analysis. In addition, an IP geophysical survey is scheduled to commence in the coming weeks in order to generate high priority drill targets.
The programme builds on extensive prior work, including:
·
Review of historical exploration datasets and non-compliant resource information
·
Previous scout drilling campaigns
·
Geological mapping and structural interpretation
·
Surface geochemical sampling and field verification
The Company believes the integration of these datasets with new geophysical data will generate multiple drill-ready targets, which will then be ranked and prioritised for future drilling programmes.
Kathleen Valley Gold Project - Strategic Review and Resource Update
At Kathleen Valley, Mila and its project level partners have initiated a strategic review of the asset, beginning with an updated resource study. Mila will participate in accordance with its existing equity position in the projects (30%) and still holds the right to earn up to 80%.
The updated study will incorporate the most recent drilling results and assess opportunities for resource growth and expansion drilling. Heritage clearance is already in place over the Coffey target area, which provides a clear pathway for future drilling, should it be prioritised following completion of the ongoing technical work.
While the majority of Mila's work is now focused on Queensland, the Company believes it retains strategic optionality and unrealised potential that targeted exploration and resource development at Kathleen Valley could unlock.
Competent Person Statement
The information in this announcement relating to Exploration Results is based on information compiled by Alastair Goodship, who is COO of Mila Resources, and a member of The Institute of Materials, Minerals and Mining and a Fellow of the Geological Society. Dr Goodship has sufficient experience relevant to the style of mineralisation and type of deposit under consideration, and to the activity he is undertaking to qualify as a Competent Person as defined in the 2012 Edition of the "Australasian Code for Reporting of Exploration Results, Mineral Resource and Ore Reserves".

latest.title: RC Results at Yarrol Continue to Derisk Project
latest.content:
28 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1118C
Mila Resources PLC
28 April 2026
Mila Resources Plc / Index: LSE / Epic: MILA / Sector: Natural Resources
28 April 2026
Mila Resources Plc
("Mila" or the "Company")
RC Results at Yarrol Continue to Derisk the Project
Mila Resources Plc (LSE: MILA), the post-discovery gold exploration accelerator, is pleased to report assay results from holes 3, 4 and 5 of the Company's 12-hole Reverse Circulation ("RC") drilling campaign completed in Q1 2026 at the Yarrol Gold Project, Queensland, Australia ("Yarrol"). The results reported below were drilled in the southern segment of the Yarrol North target.
OVERVIEW
Yarrol Gold Project - RC Drilling
·
New results from the Q1 2026 RC campaign return the highest-grade gold intercept recorded on the project at 112g/t over 1m.
·
Drillholes confirm high grade mineralisation in southernmost positions drilled to date at Yarrol North Target.
·
The results also confirm extensions of shallow gold mineralisation further to the north and continuity of mineralisation to the west that was not included in the historic resource.
·
Further results from infill and step-out holes completed as part of the Q1 2026 programme are pending and will be reported in due course.
·
The programme is designed to build the dataset required for an initial JORC mineral resource estimate ("MRE").
·
Multi-element geochemical assays are being used to refine the Company's structural interpretation of the Yarrol system and to inform next-phase drill targeting.
·
Best intercepts from the three holes include:
·
4m @ 29.4g/t gold (Au) from 8m (MYARC0222)
o
including 1m @ 112g/t Au from 10m; and
o
1m @ 5g/t Au from 11m
·
9m @ 2.48g/t Au from 31m (MYARC0216)
o
including 1m @ 15.05g/t Au from 36m
·
7m @ 1.0g/t Au from 17m (MYARC0215)
o
including 1m @ 4.04g/t Au from 21m
Mark Stephenson, Executive Chairman, commented:
"We are delighted with these results with some notably high-grade results. Every intercept, no matter the size, is helping us build tonnage and tighten the geological picture.
"What is emerging is a pattern of repeating structures hosting high-grade shoots, suggesting a more continuous and robust mineralised system than was ever previously understood. We are now moving beyond isolated results and toward the building a meaningful and economic gold resource in a proven gold district. Following our recent capital raise we will be continuing to fast track the development of this project."
Further Information
Yarrol Gold Project: Results from RC Campaign
Figure 1. An overview of Mila's completed drilling at Yarrol. Holes MYARC0222 is shown south of the historic resource area, MYARC0216 and MYARC0215 are shown at the centre and north of the historic resource area.
Mila is pleased to announce results from three further holes of the recently completed 12-hole RC programme at Yarrol.
The Q1 2026 RC programme was designed to systematically extend the mineralised footprint at Yarrol in support of an initial JORC MRE, with infill and step-out holes testing the boundaries of the system in all directions. To date, mineralisation has not been closed off in any direction and the confirmed strike length at Yarrol North now exceeds 500m.
This batch of results covers a southern step-out hole designed to test the outer limits of the system infill holes to test mineralisation continuity in the west and to the north of the historic resource area. Even at these furthest positions, broad zones of gold mineralisation with higher-grade internal intercepts have been returned, and the system remains open to the south. Further results from the remainder of the Q1 2026 RC programme are pending and will be reported in due course.
Competent Person Statement
The information in this announcement relating to Exploration Results is based on information compiled by Alastair Goodship, who is COO of Mila Resources, and a member of The Institute of Materials, Minerals and Mining and a Fellow of the Geological Society. Dr Goodship has sufficient experience relevant to the style of mineralisation and type of deposit under consideration, and to the activity he is undertaking to qualify as a Competent Person as defined in the 2012 Edition of the "Australasian Code for Reporting of Exploration Results, Mineral Resource and Ore Reserves".
**ENDS**
For more information visit www.milaresources.com or contact:
Mark Stephenson

ABOUT MILA RESOURCES
Mila Resources Plc (LSE: MILA) is a post-discovery gold exploration company focused on advancing a portfolio of gold and copper interests in Queensland, Australia. The most advanced is the Yarrol Gold Project which is moving towards an initial Mineral Resource Estimate, alongside the systematic advancement of exploration targets across its broader Queensland licence portfolio, including the Monal West porphyry corridor.

latest.title: £600,000 Placing to advance Queensland Portfolio
latest.content:
16 Apr 2026 07:30
RNS Number : 6610A
Mila Resources PLC
16 April 2026
Certain information contained within this Announcement is deemed by the Company to constitute inside information as stipulated under the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 ("MAR") as applied in the United Kingdom. Upon publication of this Announcement, this information is considered to be in the public domain.
Mila Resources Plc / Index: LSE / Epic: MILA / Sector: Natural Resources
16 April 2026
Mila Resources Plc
('Mila' or the 'Company')
Fundraise of £600,000 with existing investors to advance the Queensland Portfolio
and Total Voting Rights
Following significant expressions of interest from existing and supportive shareholders, Mila Resources Plc (LSE: MILA), the post-discovery gold exploration accelerator, announces that it has raised gross proceeds of £600,000 through a Placing of 60,000,000 New Ordinary Shares of 0.1 pence each ("Placing Shares") at a placing price of 1 pence per Placing Share (the "Placing"). Investors in the Placing will also receive one warrant per Placing Share to subscribe for one new ordinary share at an exercise price of 1.5 per share which may be exercised from 1 December 2026 until the second anniversary of Admission ("Investor Warrant").
Use of Funds
The proceeds of this Placing will be used to extend drilling operations and advance key technical studies at Yarrol, to accelerate the Company's ability to publish a maiden resource estimate. Funds will also be used to build on the so far exciting early-stage exploration at Monal to define drill targets and provide additional general working capital.
Director Dealing
Mark Stephenson, Executive Chairman, has subscribed for 5,000,000 New Ordinary Shares in the Placing for gross consideration of £50,000. Following Admission, Mark Stephenson will hold 25,073,903 Ordinary Shares being 3.4% of the enlarged share capital on Admission.
Mark Stephenson, Executive Chairman of Mila commented:
"Yarrol is rapidly defining itself as a cornerstone asset within our Queensland portfolio, and our focus is firmly on unlocking its value at pace. The placing with aligned existing shareholders ensures we have the capital to accelerate Yarrol toward a meaningful resource, where we are seeing clear indications of scale and continuity. At the same time, we are advancing the broader opportunity set, including moving the Monal copper target to drill-ready status, building on the strong results we have delivered to date."
Admission and Total Voting Rights
An application has been made for the Placing Shares to be admitted to trading on the Official List and the London Stock Exchange with effect from 8.00 a.m. on 21 April 2026 ("Admission").
In accordance with the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules, the Company confirms that following Admission, the Company's enlarged issued ordinary share capital will comprise 737,560,922 Ordinary Shares. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in Treasury. Therefore, following Admission, the above figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations to determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in the Company, under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
The Company has also issued 3,000,000 broker warrants that are exercisable at the Placing Price for a period of two years from Admission ("Broker Warrants").

company_30s.what_they_do: MILA Limited is a junior gold exploration company focused on the Yarrol project in Queensland, Australia. It drills for high‑grade gold deposits, builds resource estimates, and seeks to move the mine toward production.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Gold remains a key hedge against inflation and market volatility. Yarrol’s recent drill data suggest a continuous gold system that could supply a steady stream of high‑grade ore, positioning the company to become a niche producer in a commodity that is always in demand.
company_30s.current_state: MILA is still in the exploration phase, with no production revenue yet. The company has been raising capital through placings, has a cash burn typical of a drill‑heavy miner, and is actively refining its resource model. The share price is trading near the low end of its valuation range, reflecting the high uncertainty of a pre‑production asset.

rns_narrative: The company has been steadily moving the Yarrol project forward – from drilling start‑up in February to high‑grade intercepts announced in June. These updates reinforce the idea that the mine’s geology is continuous and scalable.

Alongside operational progress, MILA has been securing funding and positioning itself for a larger placement in April. These financial moves are aimed at extending the drill program and preparing for a resource update.

Corporate communications emphasize the strategic importance of Yarrol as a ‘continuous and scalable gold system’, signalling that management believes the project can deliver a sustainable production profile once the drilling cycle is complete.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – investors are listening for concrete drill results before committing.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls highlight the recent high‑grade intercepts and the company’s focus on a continuous gold system. They argue that a solid resource could unlock a long‑term producer.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears warn that the company still has a long way to go before it can produce, that the drill results may not translate into a mineable resource, and that capital costs could outpace the value of the deposits.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from pure skepticism to a cautious “wait‑and‑see” stance as the June results came in, but the overall volume remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for MILA is flat – no recent spikes in public interest, which is a good sign for a surprise‑worthy contrarian play.

near_term_catalysts: Release of the July resource update – if it confirms a substantial, high‑grade system, the market will likely react strongly.
Completion of the next drilling phase in August – a string of high‑grade intercepts could trigger a bounce.
Any announcement of a financing round or partnership that provides a clear path to production.
Gold price movements – a sustained rally above $2000/oz could lift the share as the company’s upside becomes more tangible.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden volume spike on the day of a drill or financing announcement, a break above the 200‑day moving average, and a clear technical support level around the current trading range.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share appears to be in the early phase of a deep crash, with the price far below any recent highs. If the APEX score suggests tightening, the next move will likely be a sharp bounce as capitulation is complete and the market digests the new data.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company sits in a deep crash zone – the share is far below its historical high and the market has largely ignored it. The recent drill results and the narrative that Yarrol is a continuous gold system are strong catalysts that could trigger a mean‑reversion rally. If the price recovers to roughly half its peak, the upside is roughly 3‑4× the current level, while the downside is capped at the present price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The mine is still unproven; a failed resource estimate or a cost‑overrun could stall the project. If the drill program fails to confirm a mineable deposit, the share could stay at its current low or fall further. The worst case is a continued decline to the lowest trading levels seen so far.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current share price, but a rebound to halfway levels could deliver a 3–4× gain. The asymmetry – small downside, large upside – makes this a classic contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 200‑day moving average or the nearest support level. Use a position size that limits risk to 2–3% of capital. Place a stop just below the support – around 10–15% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until a drill or financing announcement comes out with a clear, positive tone. If volume spikes by 30–50% on the news day and the price moves above the 200‑day MA, that is a green light.
playbook.if_passing: No immediate terminal risks, so the play remains bullish. If a delisting notice or bankruptcy filing appears, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: MILA sits in a deep crash; the next drill or financing announcement could trigger a mean‑reversion rally to halfway levels, offering a 3‑4× upside while keeping downside limited to the current price.
